skill.name: paywall-upgrade-cro
skill.description: Designs and optimizes in-app paywalls, upgrade screens, and upsell flows to convert free users to paid plans.
skill.summary: Expert guidance on paywall design, timing, copy, and A/B testing for subscription conversion.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 30 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file contains only documentation text about paywall design and conversion optimization. Bracket characters in UI mockups were misidentified as shell commands. Words like 'conversion' and 'system' triggered pattern matches but appear in benign marketing contexts. No executable code, network calls, or security risks exist.

content.value_statement: Free users are not converting to paid plans. This skill designs strategic paywalls, upgrade prompts, and upsell flows that maximize conversion while maintaining user trust.
actual_capabilities: Design paywall triggers based on user behavior and timing
Write conversion-focused copy for headlines, CTAs, and value propositions
Create tier comparison layouts and pricing displays
Plan A/B tests for paywall variants and trigger timing
Recommend mobile-specific paywall patterns for iOS and Android
Identify and avoid dark patterns and trust-destroying practices
limitations: Cannot implement actual payment processing or subscription billing
Cannot access your analytics data or conversion metrics directly
Cannot modify your application code or deploy paywall changes
Recommendations require manual implementation and testing

use_cases.title: SaaS Freemium Conversion
use_cases.description: Design feature gates and usage limit prompts to convert free users to paid plans without frustrating users or damaging retention.
use_cases.target_user: SaaS founders and product managers optimizing freemium models

use_cases.title: Mobile App Subscription
use_cases.description: Create App Store-compliant paywalls with proper trial flows, subscription tiers, and restore purchase options for iOS and Android apps.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile app developers implementing in-app purchases

use_cases.title: Trial Expiration Optimization
use_cases.description: Design trial countdown reminders, expiration warnings, and win-back flows to maximize trial-to-paid conversion rates.
use_cases.target_user: Growth marketers managing free trial programs

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paywall Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a paywall for my [product type] that gates [specific feature]. Include headline, value proposition, feature list, pricing display, and both upgrade and dismiss buttons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with paywall design for a new feature gate

prompt_templates.title: Usage Limit Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a usage limit notification for users who have hit [limit] on the free plan. Show what they lose, what upgrading provides, and offer both upgrade and alternative paths.
prompt_templates.scenario: Users reaching free tier limits need clear upgrade paths

prompt_templates.title: Trial Expiration Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a trial expiration sequence with warnings at 7 days, 3 days, and 1 day before expiration. Include what users lose, what they accomplished, and reactivation options.
prompt_templates.scenario: Maximizing trial-to-paid conversion with timely reminders

prompt_templates.title: A/B Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an A/B test plan for optimizing my paywall. Include hypotheses for trigger timing, headline variants, pricing presentation, and success metrics to track.

output_examples.input: Design a feature lock paywall for unlimited projects
output: Headline: Unlock Unlimited Projects to Build Without Limits
Value Demo: Preview showing blurred additional projects with 'Upgrade to see all your work'
Feature List: Create unlimited projects, Access project history, Export all data

output_examples.input: Create a trial expiration warning for 3 days remaining
output: Headline: Your Trial Ends in 3 Days
Accomplishments Summary: You created 12 projects, Invited 3 teammates, Exported 5 reports
What You Lose: Access to premium features, Project collaboration, Priority support

best_practices: Show paywalls after users experience value, not during onboarding
Make dismiss options clear and guilt-free to maintain trust
Test trigger timing systematically to find optimal conversion points
anti_patterns: Blocking users from core functionality before they understand the product
Hiding or shrinking the dismiss button to manipulate clicks
Showing upgrade prompts too frequently, causing user annoyance and churn

faq.question: When should I show my first paywall to new users?
faq.answer: Wait until users experience your product's 'aha moment' - typically after completing a key action or seeing value. Showing paywalls during onboarding converts poorly and increases churn.

faq.question: Should I use hard gates or soft prompts?
faq.answer: Hard gates work for feature locks where users actively request paid functionality. Soft prompts work better for general upgrade suggestions. Test both approaches for your specific context.

faq.question: How often should I show upgrade prompts?
faq.answer: Limit to 1-2 prompts per session with multi-day cool-downs after dismissal. Track annoyance signals like rage clicks or churn after paywall exposure.

faq.question: What pricing presentation converts best?
faq.answer: Show both monthly and annual options with clear savings. Annual plans typically convert at lower rates but drive more revenue. Test price prominence and framing.

faq.question: Should I offer a free trial or freemium?
faq.answer: Freemium builds larger user bases and works for products with viral potential. Free trials work better for high-value products where users need full access to evaluate. Consider your product type and market.

faq.question: How do I handle trial expiration without losing users?
faq.answer: Provide grace periods, offer downgrade paths to limited free tiers, and create win-back campaigns for expired trials. Make reactivation easy for returning users.
